content_en:
Al-Minhaj Sharh Sahih Muslim ibn al-Hajjaj (The Clear Path: Commentary on Sahih Muslim by Ibn al-Hajjaj) is an-Nawawi's comprehensive commentary on one of the two most rigorously authenticated hadith collections in Islam. Composed in the seventh Islamic century, this multi-volume work quickly established itself as the definitive commentary on Sahih Muslim and has maintained that status for nearly eight centuries.

Sahih Muslim itself requires a brief introduction to appreciate the significance of the commentary upon it. Imam Muslim ibn al-Hajjaj al-Qushayri an-Naysaburi (206–261 AH / 821–875 CE) spent fifteen years selecting and organizing the hadiths of his collection from approximately 300,000 narrations he had reviewed. His selection standards were exceptionally rigorous — some hadith scholars considered his standards even stricter than al-Bukhari's in certain respects — and his organizational methodology was distinctive: he grouped hadiths on the same subject together in a single location, rather than distributing related hadiths across multiple chapters as al-Bukhari did. This organizational choice makes Sahih Muslim more accessible for researching specific topics.

An-Nawawi was uniquely qualified to write the definitive commentary on Sahih Muslim. His mastery of hadith criticism, Shafi'i jurisprudence, Arabic linguistics, and Islamic theology — combined with his renowned piety and precision — meant that he could address every dimension of the Sahih's content with authority. He stated explicitly in his introduction that his goal was to explain the meanings of hadiths, resolve apparent contradictions, identify narrator information relevant to understanding the chain, present the legal positions of the major schools, and provide linguistic explanation of unfamiliar vocabulary.

The commentary runs to eighteen volumes in modern printed editions and is organized to follow the text of Sahih Muslim chapter by chapter, hadith by hadith. For each hadith, an-Nawawi's commentary proceeds systematically through linguistic explanation, narrator comment, legal analysis, and scholarly disagreement — creating a comprehensive guide that addresses every significant question a student or scholar might have about the text.
